business_ideas.idea: 37 Signals: self-funded SaaS business with 25-year track record, tens of millions annual profit, high margins.
Basecamp: project management software at $299/month flat for unlimited users, no per-user pricing.
HEY: email service focused on privacy and reducing email overwhelm, inbox as curation tool.
Campfire (revival): $299 one-time purchase installable chat software, own your data, unlimited users.
Once.com: umbrella brand for one-time-purchase SaaS products alternative to recurring subscription model.
We Work Remotely job board: sold to Andrew Wilkinson, now generating $4M annual profit.

predictions.text: Recurring revenue model has become exploitative; one-time purchase software will regain market share as pricing gets excessive.
Long-term thinking and founder-controlled businesses can sustain profitability better than venture-backed growth-at-all-costs.
